title: Sci-Fi Thrillers 2026
excerpt: Discover the best sci-fi thrillers of 2026
content:
Buckle up for a thrilling ride through the latest sci-fi thrillers of 2026. This year promises to be an epic adventure for fans of the genre, with a plethora of exciting releases that will keep you on the edge of your seat. From post-apocalyptic landscapes to AI-powered justice systems, these films will take you on a journey through the unknown.

First up, we have the highly anticipated sequel to the disaster movie Greenland. Greenland 2: Migration takes us on a perilous journey across a decimated Europe, where the Garrity family must fight for survival. With its stunning cinematography and gripping narrative, this film is a must-watch for fans of the sci-fi thriller genre.

But that's not all - 2026 also brings us a slew of other exciting releases, including War Machine, 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple, Mercy, and Killer Whale. Each of these films offers a unique take on the sci-fi thriller genre, with thought-provoking themes and heart-pumping action sequences. So, let's what makes each of these films special.

Greenland 2: Migration (2026)

Starring Gerard Butler, Morena Baccarin, Roman Griffin Davis
Hope is uncharted territory.

Greenland 2: Migration is a masterclass in narrative economy, with a tightly wound plot that keeps you guessing until the very end. The film's use of mise-en-scène is particularly noteworthy, with a blend of stunning landscapes and claustrophobic interiors that perfectly capture the sense of desperation and survival. One of the most striking aspects of the film is its use of diegetic sound, which adds to the overall sense of tension and realism.

The film's themes of hope and resilience in the face of overwhelming odds are both timely and timeless. The cast delivers strong performances, with Gerard Butler bringing his signature grit and determination to the role of John Garrity. The film's pacing is well-balanced, with a mix of action sequences and quieter moments that allow the audience to catch their breath.

One of the most impressive aspects of Greenland 2: Migration is its ability to balance action and emotion. The film's set pieces are both thrilling and well-choreographed, with a sense of realism that makes them all the more believable. At the same time, the film's quieter moments are filled with a sense of depth and emotion, making it easy to become invested in the characters' journeys.

Overall, Greenland 2: Migration is a must-watch for fans of the sci-fi thriller genre. With its stunning cinematography, gripping narrative, and strong performances, it's a film that will keep you on the edge of your seat from start to finish.

PickMyBinge Verdict: 6.5/10

War Machine (2026)

Starring Alan Ritchson, Dennis Quaid, Stephan James
All grit. No quit.

War Machine is a high-octane thrill ride that combines elements of science fiction and action to create a truly unforgettable experience. The film's use of special effects is seamless, with a blend of practical and CGI elements that bring the otherworldly killing machine to life in a truly terrifying way. One of the most impressive aspects of the film is its ability to balance action and suspense, with a series of set pieces that are both thrilling and well-choreographed.

The film's themes of perseverance and determination are both inspiring and thought-provoking. The cast delivers strong performances, with Alan Ritchson bringing a sense of grit and intensity to the role of the combat engineer. The film's pacing is well-balanced, with a mix of action sequences and quieter moments that allow the audience to catch their breath.

One of the most striking aspects of War Machine is its use of character foils, with a cast of characters that are both well-developed and nuanced. The film's villain is particularly noteworthy, with a sense of menace and intelligence that makes them a compelling opponent for the hero. The film's climax is both thrilling and satisfying, with a sense of resolution that ties together the various plot threads in a satisfying way.

Overall, War Machine is a must-watch for fans of science fiction and action. With its stunning special effects, gripping narrative, and strong performances, it's a film that will keep you on the edge of your seat from start to finish.

PickMyBinge Verdict: 7.2/10

28 Years Later: The Bone Temple (2026)

Starring Ralph Fiennes, Jack O'Connell, Alfie Williams
Fear is the new faith.

28 Years Later: The Bone Temple is a thought-provoking and unsettling film that combines elements of horror and science fiction to create a truly unforgettable experience. The film's use of atmospheric tension is masterful, with a blend of creepy sound design and stunning visuals that create a sense of unease and dread. One of the most impressive aspects of the film is its ability to balance horror and suspense, with a series of set pieces that are both terrifying and well-choreographed.

The film's themes of fear and faith are both timely and thought-provoking. The cast delivers strong performances, with Ralph Fiennes bringing a sense of gravity and intensity to the role of Dr. Kelson. The film's pacing is well-balanced, with a mix of action sequences and quieter moments that allow the audience to catch their breath.

One of the most striking aspects of 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple is its use of symbolism, with a series of images and motifs that add depth and complexity to the narrative. The film's climax is both thrilling and satisfying, with a sense of resolution that ties together the various plot threads in a satisfying way.

Overall, 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple is a must-watch for fans of horror and science fiction. With its stunning visuals, gripping narrative, and strong performances, it's a film that will keep you on the edge of your seat from start to finish.

PickMyBinge Verdict: 7.2/10

Mercy (2026)

Starring Chris Pratt, Rebecca Ferguson, Kali Reis
Prove your innocence to an AI judge or face execution.

Mercy is a gripping and thought-provoking film that combines elements of science fiction and thriller to create a truly unforgettable experience. The film's use of social commentary is timely and thought-provoking, with a series of themes and motifs that add depth and complexity to the narrative. One of the most impressive aspects of the film is its ability to balance action and suspense, with a series of set pieces that are both thrilling and well-choreographed.

The film's themes of justice and morality are both timely and thought-provoking. The cast delivers strong performances, with Chris Pratt bringing a sense of charm and intensity to the role of the detective. The film's pacing is well-balanced, with a mix of action sequences and quieter moments that allow the audience to catch their breath.

One of the most striking aspects of Mercy is its use of technology as a plot device, with a series of twists and turns that keep the audience guessing until the very end. The film's climax is both thrilling and satisfying, with a sense of resolution that ties together the various plot threads in a satisfying way.

Overall, Mercy is a must-watch for fans of science fiction and thriller. With its gripping narrative, strong performances, and thought-provoking themes, it's a film that will keep you on the edge of your seat from start to finish.

PickMyBinge Verdict: 7.0/10

Killer Whale (2026)

Starring Virginia Gardner, Mel Jarnson, Mitchell Hope
It's feeding time.

Killer Whale is a tense and suspenseful film that combines elements of thriller and horror to create a truly unforgettable experience. The film's use of atmospheric tension is masterful, with a blend of creepy sound design and stunning visuals that create a sense of unease and dread. One of the most impressive aspects of the film is its ability to balance horror and suspense, with a series of set pieces that are both terrifying and well-choreographed.

The film's themes of survival and perseverance are both timely and thought-provoking. The cast delivers strong performances, with Virginia Gardner bringing a sense of charm and intensity to the role of Maddie. The film's pacing is well-balanced, with a mix of action sequences and quieter moments that allow the audience to catch their breath.

One of the most striking aspects of Killer Whale is its use of setting as a character, with a remote lagoon that becomes a character in its own right. The film's climax is both thrilling and satisfying, with a sense of resolution that ties together the various plot threads in a satisfying way.

Overall, Killer Whale is a must-watch for fans of thriller and horror. With its stunning visuals, gripping narrative, and strong performances, it's a film that will keep you on the edge of your seat from start to finish.

PickMyBinge Verdict: 5.7/10
